Reach the end of each level while avoiding hazards and solving physics-based puzzles. Collect asterisks (*) for points and hit checkpoints to save your progress. Fall into gaps or touch spikes, and you'll respawn at your last checkpoint. Complete all levels to master the history of typography! This test verifies that the two connected dots can move left and right properly, jump with realistic physics, and that the Matter.js physics engine is working correctly. The test will perform basic movement commands to ensure responsive controls. Start the game and perform a sequence of movements: move right for 2 seconds, jump twice to test double jump, move left for 2 seconds, and jump again. This tests horizontal movement in both directions, single jump, and double jump mechanics. The test runs for approximately 10 seconds. The player should move smoothly in response to inputs, jumps should have appropriate height and gravity, double jump should work mid-air, and the two dots should remain connected throughout. The physics should feel natural with proper acceleration and deceleration. Test is successful if all movements execute without errors. Testing level completion and win condition. This test automates gameplay to reach the end of the first level, triggering the win state. It verifies that the game can detect when the player reaches the goal, properly transitions to the next level or win screen, and handles level progression correctly. Start the game and perform continuous right movement with periodic jumps to navigate the level terrain. The bot will jump at regular intervals to clear obstacles and platforms. Movement continues until the level end is reached or 30 seconds elapse. This simulates a player completing the level. The player should successfully navigate through the first level, reach the goal area at the end, and trigger the level complete state. The game should show completion feedback and either load the next level or show a win screen. Test succeeds when gamePhase changes to indicate level completion without crashes. Testing checkpoint system and respawn mechanics. This test verifies that checkpoints save player progress and that respawning after falling into gaps or hitting hazards works correctly. The player should return to the last activated checkpoint with their position reset properly. Start the game, move right to activate a checkpoint, then deliberately fall into a gap by moving off a platform edge. After respawning, verify the player returns to the checkpoint location. Repeat this process multiple times to ensure consistent behavior. After falling, the player should respawn at the last checkpoint position rather than the level start. The respawn should be instant with proper physics state reset. Health or lives (if implemented) should decrease appropriately. Test succeeds if respawn location is correct and no physics glitches occur. Testing collision detection with typographic platforms and hazards. Navigate through the level by jumping onto various letter platforms. Perform jumps at different angles to test edge cases. The player should land solidly on all platforms regardless of their typographic shape, with no falling through or getting stuck. Collision with hazards should trigger appropriate damage or death response. Test succeeds if all collisions are detected accurately and physics remain stable. Testing pause functionality and game state management. Type:Rider - Typography Platformer

Navigate two connected rolling dots through intricate typographic worlds! Each level is constructed from letters, symbols, and fonts representing different eras of typography. Reach the end of each level while avoiding hazards and solving physics-based puzzles. Collect asterisks (*) for points and hit checkpoints to save your progress. Fall into gaps or touch spikes, and you'll respawn at your last checkpoint. Complete all levels to master the history of typography!

Controls: Arrow Left/A: Move left | Arrow Right/D: Move right | Space/W: Jump (press again in mid-air for double jump) | Enter: Start game | Esc: Pause/Unpause | R: Restart from GAME_OVER

TEST_1.test_description: Testing basic movement mechanics and physics. This test verifies that the two connected dots can move left and right properly, jump with realistic physics, and that the Matter.js physics engine is working correctly. The test will perform basic movement commands to ensure responsive controls.
TEST_1.strategy_description: Start the game and perform a sequence of movements: move right for 2 seconds, jump twice to test double jump, move left for 2 seconds, and jump again. This tests horizontal movement in both directions, single jump, and double jump mechanics. The test runs for approximately 10 seconds.
TEST_1.expected_outcome: The player should move smoothly in response to inputs, jumps should have appropriate height and gravity, double jump should work mid-air, and the two dots should remain connected throughout. The physics should feel natural with proper acceleration and deceleration. Test is successful if all movements execute without errors.

TEST_3.strategy_description: Start the game, move right to activate a checkpoint, then deliberately fall into a gap by moving off a platform edge. After respawning, verify the player returns to the checkpoint location. Repeat this process multiple times to ensure consistent behavior.
TEST_3.expected_outcome: After falling, the player should respawn at the last checkpoint position rather than the level start. The respawn should be instant with proper physics state reset. Health or lives (if implemented) should decrease appropriately. Test succeeds if respawn location is correct and no physics glitches occur.

test_7_ModeBtn.expected: Blocks should move realistically when pushed, with appropriate friction and weight. The player should be able to jump onto pushed blocks to access new areas. Switches should activate with visual feedback when the player lands on them. Test succeeds if all puzzle mechanics work smoothly without physics glitches.
